title: Home Economics Education and Hands-On Cooking Skills Among Learners in the Kaputian District of Igacos
author: Garrido, Janice
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The study aimed to determine the significant relationship between home economics education and hands-on cooking skills among learners. It employed the descriptive quantitative correlational method. The researcher used a random sampling method to conduct the study, selecting a proportional number of respondents from every public national high school in the Kaputian district, Division of Igacos Island Garden City of Samal, and everyone was chosen entirely by drawing lots. Due to the large population of learners in public schools in the Kaputian district, Division of Island Garden City of Samal, anyone can be chosen during the sampling process. The findings revealed that the extent of home economics education in terms of knowledge and awareness, cooking skills and practices, and health outcomes was extensive. The extent to which hands-on cooking skills among learners enhanced nutritional knowledge, changed dietary behaviors and fostered positive attitudes toward cooking and healthy eating was extensive. There was a significant relationship between home economics education and Hands-On Cooking Skills Among Learners. Research indicates that students enrolled in home economics programs demonstrate high cooking skills, improved problem-solving abilities, and enhanced sewing skills for economic empowerment. They may then continue to participate in school activities promoting their cooking skills.
